Iteration 0: Base program full valset score: 0.9482758620689655 over 58 / 58 examples
Iteration 1: Selected program 0 score: 0.9482758620689655

[1;31mGive Feedback / Get Help: https://github.com/BerriAI/litellm/issues/new[0m

Iteration 1: Exception during reflection/proposal: litellm.RateLimitError: RateLimitError: OpenAIException - Too many requests. For more on scraping GitHub and how it may affect your rights, please review our Terms of Service (https://docs.github.com/en/site-policy/github-terms/github-terms-of-service).

  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/litellm/llms/openai/openai.py", line 852, in completion

  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/litellm/llms/openai/openai.py", line 780, in completion

        ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^
        openai_client=openai_client,
        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
    ...<2 lines>...
        logging_obj=logging_obj,
        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
    )
    ^
  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/litellm/litellm_core_utils/logging_utils.py", line 344, in sync_wrapper

  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/litellm/llms/openai/openai.py", line 503, in make_sync_openai_chat_completion_request
    raise e
  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/litellm/llms/openai/openai.py", line 478, in make_sync_openai_chat_completion_request
    raw_response = openai_client.chat.completions.with_raw_response.create(
        **data, timeout=timeout
    )
  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/openai/_legacy_response.py", line 367, in wrapped
    return cast(LegacyAPIResponse[R], func(*args, **kwargs))
                                      ~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/openai/_utils/_utils.py", line 286, in wrapper
    return func(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/openai/resources/chat/completions/completions.py", line 1211, in create

           ~~~~~~~~~~^

        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
    ...<47 lines>...

        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

    ^

                           ~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/openai/_base_client.py", line 1070, in request

    )

  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/litellm/litellm_core_utils/exception_mapping_utils.py", line 579, in exception_type
    raise RateLimitError(
    ...<5 lines>...
    )
litellm.exceptions.RateLimitError: litellm.RateLimitError: RateLimitError: OpenAIException - Too many requests. For more on scraping GitHub and how it may affect your rights, please review our Terms of Service (https://docs.github.com/en/site-policy/github-terms/github-terms-of-service).

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/litellm/llms/openai/openai.py", line 852, in completion

  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/litellm/llms/openai/openai.py", line 780, in completion

        ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^
        openai_client=openai_client,
        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
    ...<2 lines>...
        logging_obj=logging_obj,
        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
    )
    ^
  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/litellm/litellm_core_utils/logging_utils.py", line 344, in sync_wrapper

  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/litellm/llms/openai/openai.py", line 503, in make_sync_openai_chat_completion_request
    raise e
  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/litellm/llms/openai/openai.py", line 478, in make_sync_openai_chat_completion_request
    raw_response = openai_client.chat.completions.with_raw_response.create(
        **data, timeout=timeout
    )
  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/openai/_legacy_response.py", line 367, in wrapped
    return cast(LegacyAPIResponse[R], func(*args, **kwargs))
                                      ~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/openai/_utils/_utils.py", line 286, in wrapper
    return func(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/openai/resources/chat/completions/completions.py", line 1211, in create

           ~~~~~~~~~~^

        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
    ...<47 lines>...
        stream_cls=Stream[ChatCompletionChunk],
        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
    )
    ^

                           ~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/openai/_base_client.py", line 1070, in request

  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/litellm/utils.py", line 1769, in wrapper
    raise e
  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/litellm/utils.py", line 1590, in wrapper
    result = original_function(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/litellm/main.py", line 4411, in completion
    raise exception_type(
          ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^
        model=model,
        ^^^^^^^^^^^^
    ...<3 lines>...
        extra_kwargs=kwargs,
        ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
    )
    ^
  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/litellm/litellm_core_utils/exception_mapping_utils.py", line 2456, in exception_type
    raise e
  File "/usr/local/python/current/lib/python3.14/site-packages/litellm/litellm_core_utils/exception_mapping_utils.py", line 579, in exception_type
    raise RateLimitError(
    ...<5 lines>...
    )
litellm.exceptions.RateLimitError: litellm.RateLimitError: RateLimitError: OpenAIException - Too many requests. For more on scraping GitHub and how it may affect your rights, please review our Terms of Service (https://docs.github.com/en/site-policy/github-terms/github-terms-of-service).

Iteration 8: Reflective mutation did not propose a new candidate
{
  "skill": "mermaid",
  "original": "# Mermaid Diagrams\n\nSkill for generating Mermaid diagrams embedded in markdown fences. Mermaid is used\nfor inline documentation diagrams \u2014 flowcharts, sequences, state machines, ER\ndiagrams, and Gantt charts. For architecture diagrams with Azure service icons,\nuse the `drawio` skill instead.\n\n## When to Use Mermaid\n\n- Inline diagrams inside markdown documents (`.md`, `.mdx`)\n- Quick flowcharts for operational runbooks and process docs\n- Sequence diagrams for auth flows and API interactions\n- Gantt charts for project plans and maintenance schedules\n- State diagrams for lifecycle documentation\n- ER diagrams for data model overviews\n- Azure resource relationship diagrams from live queries\n\n## Syntax Reference\n\n### Flowcharts\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    A[\"Step 1\"] --> B{\"Decision\"}\n    B -->|\"Yes\"| C[\"Action\"]\n    B -->|\"No\"| D[\"Skip\"]\n```\n\nUse `graph TB` (top-to-bottom) for vertical layouts.\nUse `graph LR` (left-to-right) for horizontal layouts.\nUse subgraphs for logical grouping:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Resource Group\"\n        APP[\"App Service\"]\n        SQL[\"SQL Database\"]\n    end\n    APP --> SQL\n```\n\n### Sequence Diagrams\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client\n    participant API\n    participant DB\n    Client->>API: Request\n    API->>DB: Query\n    DB-->>API: Result\n    API-->>Client: Response\n```\n\n### Gantt Charts\n\n```mermaid\ngantt\n    title Deployment Schedule\n    dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD\n    section Phase 1\n        Task A :a1, 2026-01-01, 7d\n        Task B :a2, after a1, 5d\n```\n\n### State Diagrams\n\n```mermaid\nstateDiagram-v2\n    [*] --> Pending\n    Pending --> Active: Approve\n    Active --> Suspended: Suspend\n    Suspended --> Active: Resume\n    Active --> [*]: Complete\n```\n\n### ER Diagrams\n\n```mermaid\nerDiagram\n    CUSTOMER ||--o{ ORDER : places\n    ORDER ||--|{ LINE_ITEM : contains\n    PRODUCT ||--o{ LINE_ITEM : \"ordered in\"\n```\n\n## Theming (Dark Mode Compatible)\n\nInclude a neutral theme directive for dark mode compatibility:\n\n```mermaid\n%%{\n  init: {\n    'theme': 'base',\n    'themeVariables': {\n      'primaryColor': '#ffffff',\n      'primaryTextColor': '#333333',\n      'primaryBorderColor': '#e91e63',\n      'lineColor': '#475569',\n      'fontFamily': 'ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif'\n    }\n  }\n}%%\ngraph LR\n    A --> B\n```\n\n## Node Styling\n\nUse `classDef` for consistent node styling:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    classDef default fill:#ffffff,stroke:#e91e63,stroke-width:2px,color:#1f2937,rx:8px,ry:8px;\n    classDef gate fill:#ffffff,stroke:#3b82f6,stroke-width:2px,color:#1f2937,rx:8px,ry:8px;\n\n    S1[\"Step 1\"]\n    G1{{\"Gate\"}}:::gate\n```\n\n## Azure Resource Visualization\n\nFor visualizing live Azure resource groups as Mermaid diagrams, use the\n`azure-resources` skill (Mode B: Visualize) which outputs resource relationship\ndiagrams in Mermaid format. That skill handles Azure Resource Graph queries,\nresource discovery, and relationship mapping.\n\n### Resource Diagram Conventions\n\n- Group by layer: Network, Compute, Data, Security, Monitoring\n- Include resource details in node labels (use `<br/>` for line breaks)\n- Label all connections descriptively\n- Use subgraphs for logical grouping\n- Connection types:\n  - `-->` for data flow or dependencies\n  - `-.->` for optional/conditional connections\n  - `==>` for critical/primary paths\n\n## Astro / Starlight Integration\n\nIn this project, Mermaid is rendered client-side by `rehype-mermaid-lite`.\nUse fenced code blocks with `mermaid` language:\n\n````markdown\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n  A --> B\n```\n````\n\n## Rules\n\n**DO:** Use fenced code blocks with `mermaid` language tag \u00b7 Include theme\ndirectives for dark mode \u00b7 Use `graph TB` for vertical layouts \u00b7 Use subgraphs\nfor grouping \u00b7 Use descriptive connection labels \u00b7 Validate syntax before\ncommitting.\n\n**DON'T:** Use Mermaid for WAF/cost charts (use `python-diagrams`) \u00b7 Use Mermaid\nfor primary architecture diagrams with Azure icons (use `drawio`) \u00b7 Omit\ntheme directives \u00b7 Create overly complex diagrams that don't render well \u00b7\nUse inline Mermaid for diagrams that need icon embedding.\n\n## Steps\n\n1. **Pick the diagram type** \u2014 flowchart, sequence, Gantt, state, ER, class \u2014 see [Syntax Reference](#syntax-reference)\n2. **Choose the layout** \u2014 `graph TB` (vertical) or `graph LR` (horizontal); use subgraphs for logical grouping\n3. **Author the diagram** inside a triple-backtick `mermaid` fence in your markdown\n4. **Add theming** \u2014 include dark-mode-compatible theme directives (see [Theming](#theming-dark-mode-compatible))\n5. **Apply node styling** \u2014 use descriptive labels and consistent shapes for like roles\n6. **Validate** \u2014 render in VS Code preview or Starlight build; check that all nodes resolve and edges have labels\n7. **Commit** \u2014 the rendered Mermaid stays inline; no separate `.drawio` / `.png` artifact needed\n\n## Scope Exclusions\n\nDoes NOT: generate Draw.io architecture diagrams \u00b7 produce Python charts \u00b7\ngenerate Bicep/Terraform \u00b7 create ADRs \u00b7 deploy resources \u00b7 embed Azure service\nicons (use `drawio` skill).",
  "optimized": "---\nname: mermaid\ndescription: '**UTILITY SKILL** \u2014 Mermaid diagram generation for inline markdown documentation: flowcharts, sequence diagrams, Gantt, class, state, ER. WHEN: \"mermaid flowchart\", \"sequence diagram\", \"Gantt chart\", \"state diagram\", \"ER diagram\", \"inline markdown diagram\". USE FOR: inline markdown diagrams, flowcharts, sequence diagrams, Gantt charts, state diagrams, ER diagrams. DO NOT USE FOR: architecture diagrams with Azure icons (use drawio), WAF/cost charts (use python-diagrams).'\ncompatibility: Works with VS Code Copilot, Claude Code, and any tool that renders Mermaid in markdown.\nlicense: MIT\nmetadata:\n  author: apex\n  version: \"1.0\"\n---\n\n# Mermaid Diagrams\n\nSkill for generating Mermaid diagrams embedded in markdown fences. Mermaid is used\nfor inline documentation diagrams \u2014 flowcharts, sequences, state machines, ER\ndiagrams, and Gantt charts. For architecture diagrams with Azure service icons,\nuse the `drawio` skill instead.\n\n## When to Use Mermaid\n\n- Inline diagrams inside markdown documents (`.md`, `.mdx`)\n- Quick flowcharts for operational runbooks and process docs\n- Sequence diagrams for auth flows and API interactions\n- Gantt charts for project plans and maintenance schedules\n- State diagrams for lifecycle documentation\n- ER diagrams for data model overviews\n- Azure resource relationship diagrams from live queries\n\n## Syntax Reference\n\n### Flowcharts\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    A[\"Step 1\"] --> B{\"Decision\"}\n    B -->|\"Yes\"| C[\"Action\"]\n    B -->|\"No\"| D[\"Skip\"]\n```\n\nUse `graph TB` (top-to-bottom) for vertical layouts.\nUse `graph LR` (left-to-right) for horizontal layouts.\nUse subgraphs for logical grouping:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    subgraph \"Resource Group\"\n        APP[\"App Service\"]\n        SQL[\"SQL Database\"]\n    end\n    APP --> SQL\n```\n\n### Sequence Diagrams\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n    participant Client\n    participant API\n    participant DB\n    Client->>API: Request\n    API->>DB: Query\n    DB-->>API: Result\n    API-->>Client: Response\n```\n\n### Gantt Charts\n\n```mermaid\ngantt\n    title Deployment Schedule\n    dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD\n    section Phase 1\n        Task A :a1, 2026-01-01, 7d\n        Task B :a2, after a1, 5d\n```\n\n### State Diagrams\n\n```mermaid\nstateDiagram-v2\n    [*] --> Pending\n    Pending --> Active: Approve\n    Active --> Suspended: Suspend\n    Suspended --> Active: Resume\n    Active --> [*]: Complete\n```\n\n### ER Diagrams\n\n```mermaid\nerDiagram\n    CUSTOMER ||--o{ ORDER : places\n    ORDER ||--|{ LINE_ITEM : contains\n    PRODUCT ||--o{ LINE_ITEM : \"ordered in\"\n```\n\n## Theming (Dark Mode Compatible)\n\nInclude a neutral theme directive for dark mode compatibility:\n\n```mermaid\n%%{\n  init: {\n    'theme': 'base',\n    'themeVariables': {\n      'primaryColor': '#ffffff',\n      'primaryTextColor': '#333333',\n      'primaryBorderColor': '#e91e63',\n      'lineColor': '#475569',\n      'fontFamily': 'ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, sans-serif'\n    }\n  }\n}%%\ngraph LR\n    A --> B\n```\n\n## Node Styling\n\nUse `classDef` for consistent node styling:\n\n```mermaid\ngraph TB\n    classDef default fill:#ffffff,stroke:#e91e63,stroke-width:2px,color:#1f2937,rx:8px,ry:8px;\n    classDef gate fill:#ffffff,stroke:#3b82f6,stroke-width:2px,color:#1f2937,rx:8px,ry:8px;\n\n    S1[\"Step 1\"]\n    G1{{\"Gate\"}}:::gate\n```\n\n## Azure Resource Visualization\n\nFor visualizing live Azure resource groups as Mermaid diagrams, use the\n`azure-resources` skill (Mode B: Visualize) which outputs resource relationship\ndiagrams in Mermaid format. That skill handles Azure Resource Graph queries,\nresource discovery, and relationship mapping.\n\n### Resource Diagram Conventions\n\n- Group by layer: Network, Compute, Data, Security, Monitoring\n- Include resource details in node labels (use `<br/>` for line breaks)\n- Label all connections descriptively\n- Use subgraphs for logical grouping\n- Connection types:\n  - `-->` for data flow or dependencies\n  - `-.->` for optional/conditional connections\n  - `==>` for critical/primary paths\n\n## Astro / Starlight Integration\n\nIn this project, Mermaid is rendered client-side by `rehype-mermaid-lite`.\nUse fenced code blocks with `mermaid` language:\n\n````markdown\n```mermaid\ngraph LR\n  A --> B\n```\n````\n\n## Rules\n\n**DO:** Use fenced code blocks with `mermaid` language tag \u00b7 Include theme\ndirectives for dark mode \u00b7 Use `graph TB` for vertical layouts \u00b7 Use subgraphs\nfor grouping \u00b7 Use descriptive connection labels \u00b7 Validate syntax before\ncommitting.\n\n**DON'T:** Use Mermaid for WAF/cost charts (use `python-diagrams`) \u00b7 Use Mermaid\nfor primary architecture diagrams with Azure icons (use `drawio`) \u00b7 Omit\ntheme directives \u00b7 Create overly complex diagrams that don't render well \u00b7\nUse inline Mermaid for diagrams that need icon embedding.\n\n## Steps\n\n1. **Pick the diagram type** \u2014 flowchart, sequence, Gantt, state, ER, class \u2014 see [Syntax Reference](#syntax-reference)\n2. **Choose the layout** \u2014 `graph TB` (vertical) or `graph LR` (horizontal); use subgraphs for logical grouping\n3. **Author the diagram** inside a triple-backtick `mermaid` fence in your markdown\n4. **Add theming** \u2014 include dark-mode-compatible theme directives (see [Theming](#theming-dark-mode-compatible))\n5. **Apply node styling** \u2014 use descriptive labels and consistent shapes for like roles\n6. **Validate** \u2014 render in VS Code preview or Starlight build; check that all nodes resolve and edges have labels\n7. **Commit** \u2014 the rendered Mermaid stays inline; no separate `.drawio` / `.png` artifact needed\n\n## Scope Exclusions\n\nDoes NOT: generate Draw.io architecture diagrams \u00b7 produce Python charts \u00b7\ngenerate Bicep/Terraform \u00b7 create ADRs \u00b7 deploy resources \u00b7 embed Azure service\nicons (use `drawio` skill).\n",
  "best_score": null
}
